config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama; kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa kontrol, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan, suara minoritas, dan martabat orang per orang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan proporsional, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar secara sembarangan. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang melindungi individu sekaligus memungkinkan masyarakat berjalan baik.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should matter, but I do not think its interests should automatically come before individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize collective stability and public order, especially when social harmony and long-term development are at stake, but I also think that without real protections for the individual, “society” can become a convenient excuse for abuse. The strongest arrangement is not sacrificing rights for the collective, but setting clear limits so public interests are pursued in a fair and proportional way. If individual dignity is treated as secondary too often, the society itself becomes less trustworthy and less healthy.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya paham betul alasan orang menekankan gotong royong, ketertiban, dan harmoni sosial, tetapi pengalaman kita juga menunjukkan bahwa atas nama “kepentingan umum” hak minoritas dan kebebasan warga bisa mudah disingkirkan. Justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang punya batas jelas: kepentingan bersama boleh dibatasi, tapi hak dasar tidak boleh dijadikan korban rutin atau alasan yang serba bisa. Menurut saya, prioritas yang benar bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang tegas dengan perlindungan hak sebagai pagar utamanya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of collective stability, especially in a country where order and long-term development are often valued highly, but I also think that once “society” is treated as the higher authority in every case, it can easily become a vague excuse for suppressing people. Rights need to be real limits, not flexible suggestions, otherwise the individual becomes too weak to resist abuse. A healthy society is not one that simply wins over the person, but one that protects both public interests and basic dignity at the same time.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh diposisikan lebih tinggi secara otomatis daripada hak individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya paham nilai gotong royong dan ketertiban, tetapi saya juga melihat betapa sering alasan “demi umum” dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau mengabaikan prosedur yang adil. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas supaya tidak berubah jadi alat pembenaran kekuasaan. Jadi, yang benar menurut saya bukan mendahulukan masyarakat atas individu, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan hak individu sebagai pagar utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should routinely come before the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective goals, but I have also seen how easily “the greater good” can be used to silence ordinary people and make rights feel optional. Public interests matter, yet they should be pursued within firm limits, because once individual dignity is treated as secondary, the society itself becomes less trustworthy. A strong society is one that can protect common interests without turning people into sacrifices.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
